# FreeMarketWatch

> Purchasing power is the only honest benchmark. Not the dollar, not Bitcoin, not gold. The question for any asset, currency, or good is: does it maintain, gain, or lose purchasing power over time?

FreeMarketWatch is an economics education and data dashboard that measures financial assets against THM — Theoretical Hard Money — instead of the US dollar. Every chart on the site shows the same markets, the same prices, the same assets you see elsewhere. The lens is different.

The site has two audiences: people who understand markets and want to see reality unfiltered by fiat framing, and people who are learning what money is and why debasement matters.

## What is THM?

THM (Theoretical Hard Money) is a synthetic benchmark computed from 111 years of US M2 money supply and GDP data. It represents what purchasing power would look like under a fixed-supply monetary standard — the end state of Bitcoin as a mature monetary standard, with the adoption-phase speculation factored out.

Formula: `THM(t) = 100 × (M2_t / GDP_t) / (M2_start / GDP_start)`

Everything above THM on a chart is gaining real purchasing power. Everything below THM is losing it. The dashed green line on every chart is THM.

This is not a Bitcoin price site. Bitcoin appears as a data series and as the strongest historical example of hard money properties. The hero of the site is honest purchasing power measurement.

## Pages

- [Home](https://freemarketwatch.world/): Hook page — the site's opening argument for why the monetary lens matters
- [Dashboard](https://freemarketwatch.world/dashboard): Live purchasing-power charts for currencies, risk-off assets, and risk-on assets measured against THM. Three panels (Currency, Risk-Off, Risk-On), 1/5/10-year windows, BTC classification toggle. Data updated daily.
- [About](https://freemarketwatch.world/about): Site premise, the two founding assumptions (purchasing power as the only honest benchmark; THM as Bitcoin's mature end state), how to read the charts, data sources
- [The Lens](https://freemarketwatch.world/lens): Hub for the intellectual framework behind the site — four components

## The Lens

The Lens is the intellectual foundation of the site. Four components:

- [Why the Fiat Lens Distorts](https://freemarketwatch.world/lens/fiat): Six-act series on money, debasement, and the hard money alternative. Builds from first principles to Bitcoin. ~30 minutes total.
  - [Act 1: Why Money Exists](https://freemarketwatch.world/lens/fiat/act/1): From barter to Bitcoin — the origin story of money
  - [Act 2: What Makes Good Money](https://freemarketwatch.world/lens/fiat/act/2): Properties that separate honest money from instruments that fail at their job
  - [Act 3: The Blast Radius](https://freemarketwatch.world/lens/fiat/act/3): How fiat debasement works in practice
  - [Act 4: Hard Money Changes Everything](https://freemarketwatch.world/lens/fiat/act/4): How bad money distorts investment, destroys savings, warps economic decision-making
  - [Act 5: Bitcoin](https://freemarketwatch.world/lens/fiat/act/5): Bitcoin as sound money — fixed supply, no authority
  - [Act 6: What Comes Next](https://freemarketwatch.world/lens/fiat/act/6): The path forward under hard money assumptions
- [The THM Lens](https://freemarketwatch.world/lens/thm): Full methodology — what THM is, how it is computed, three measurement approaches (CPI-based, M2/GDP-based, M2-raw), why M2/GDP is preferred, honest acknowledgment of open questions. Includes four research charts comparing methods over 111 years.
- [Investing Through the THM Lens](https://freemarketwatch.world/lens/investing): How investment analysis changes when hard money is the base position. First-principles framework; dashboard tools in development.
- [Bitcoin Adoption Index](https://freemarketwatch.world/lens/adoption): In development. Will track Bitcoin's transition from asset to monetary standard via Lightning Network volume, merchant acceptance, and payment flows.

## Data Sources and Methodology

- **Purchasing power deflator**: US M2/GDP ratio (FRED M2SL + GDPC1), annual data linearly interpolated to monthly
- **USD series**: `100 × (M2GDP_start / M2GDP_t)` — exact inverse of THM
- **Other currencies** (EUR, JPY, GBP, CNY): `100 × (FX_start / FX_t) × (M2GDP_start / M2GDP_t)` using FRED FX data
- **Equities and ETFs**: `100 × (price_t / price_start) × (M2GDP_start / M2GDP_t)` — nominal price change adjusted by M2/GDP
- **Bitcoin**: Same formula as equities; source CryptoCompare; data from 2010-07-17
- **THM**: M2/GDP-based primary formula; CPI-based and M2-raw variants available on /lens/thm for comparison only
- Pre-FRED historical data (1913–1959) from Friedman & Schwartz / BEA

## Contact

- [Contact](https://freemarketwatch.world/contact): Get in touch — corrections, extensions, disagreements with the argument are welcome

---

*Not financial advice. Data updated daily. FreeMarketWatch · 2026*
